A Photo Series

Thames — Morning Light

Shot on iPhone · London, England
Eighteen Photographs

Eighteen frames taken along the Thames on mornings of varying light and weather — from dense yellow fog to stormy overcast skies. The river, its bridges, and the distant silhouettes of Tower Bridge and Canary Wharf serve as constant anchors while the atmosphere around them shifts, each morning a different mood on the same stage.

001
#001

Millennium Bridge — Yellow Haze

The elegant sweep of the Millennium Bridge cuts across a Thames bathed in dense yellow-green morning fog. Tower Bridge is a ghostly outline in the mid-distance, with Canary Wharf's towers barely visible above the mist. A lone jogger crosses the bridge. The sun, a perfect white disc, burns through the haze in the upper right.

File  IMG_2554.JPG

002
#002

Millennium Bridge — Closer Frame

A tighter composition on the Millennium Bridge, foregrounding its distinctive suspension cables and central pylon. The fog has thickened slightly, making Tower Bridge and the Canary Wharf skyline even more spectral. The pale sulphur-yellow sky renders the entire scene as if painted in watercolour washes.

File  IMG_2555.JPG

003
#003

Millennium Bridge — Deepest Fog

The densest fog of the series. The Millennium Bridge fills the lower third of the frame while Tower Bridge and the City skyline dissolve almost completely into a uniform pale lemon haze. The river surface is glassy and still, perfectly mirroring the diffuse morning light.

File  IMG_2556.JPG

004
#004

Millennium Bridge — Clearing Mist

The fog begins to lift, shifting the palette from yellow-green to a cooler blue-grey. The Millennium Bridge is now fully visible, its walkway busy with early commuters. Southwark Cathedral's dome is visible to the left, and Tower Bridge stands with slightly more definition against a brightening sky.

File  IMG_2590.JPG

005
#005

Southwark Bridge — Jade Morning

Shot from a higher vantage, this frame takes in Southwark Bridge in the foreground with the Millennium Bridge visible beyond. Two cormorants wheel above the river mid-frame. The cool jade-green atmospheric haze softens the entire skyline into layered planes of tone.

File  IMG_2671.JPG

006
#006

Southwark Bridge — Sun Climbing

The sun has risen higher, burning through the jade haze with more intensity. Southwark Bridge is rendered in near-silhouette, pedestrians moving across it like small dark figures. Canary Wharf and Tower Bridge sit in clearly defined mid-ground layers behind the bridge.

File  IMG_2672.JPG

007
#007

Southwark Bridge — Deep Silhouette

The darkest and most graphic of the Southwark Bridge frames. The bridge and its surroundings drop to near black silhouette against a glowing teal-green sky. Tower Bridge's two towers are sharply defined in the distance, and the river shimmers with reflected light.

File  IMG_2673.JPG

008
#008

Southwark Bridge — River Traffic

A river taxi passes beneath Southwark Bridge, trailing a gentle wake. This is the busiest the Thames has appeared in the series, with morning boat traffic beginning to pick up. The green-grey haze persists but the overall exposure is lighter, revealing more detail in the South Bank buildings.

File  IMG_2674.JPG

009
#009

Southwark Bridge — Barge Passing

A wide, flat-hulled barge moves east through the foreground, dwarfing the smaller vessels. The sun has climbed to upper-right, casting long silver reflections across the water. Tower Bridge remains a constant mid-distance anchor point while Canary Wharf rises to the left behind a veil of haze.

File  IMG_2675.JPG

010
#010

Southwark Bridge — Barge Close

The same barge captured again, now positioned more centrally in the frame. The composition is balanced — barge in the foreground, Southwark Bridge in the middle ground, Tower Bridge and the skyline behind. The teal-green palette is consistent throughout, giving the image a monochrome quality despite being in colour.

File  IMG_2676.JPG

011
#011

Millennium Bridge — Overcast Revival

The palette shifts markedly — cloud cover has built, reducing the warm haze to a cool, flat blue-grey light. The Millennium Bridge is front and centre, its distinctive steel tensioned deck and suspension rods clearly visible. Canary Wharf's skyline has sharpened considerably with the improved clarity.

File  IMG_2700.JPG

012
#012

Thames Panorama — High Contrast B&W

The sole black-and-white image in the collection. Shot on an overcast day with dramatic cloud formations, the entire scene is rendered in pure tones of silver and charcoal. The sun blazes behind the clouds creating a radiant backlit halo. Canary Wharf and Tower Bridge stand as bold silhouettes with no colour to soften them.

File  IMG_2701.jpg

013
#013

Millennium Bridge — Pale Winter Light

A cold, flat winter morning. The Thames is silvery and choppy, and the sky is a pale wash of dove grey with a faint yellow tinge at the horizon. The Millennium Bridge is captured mid-span, and the whole scene has a quiet, unhurried quality — the city yet to fully wake.

File  IMG_2702.jpg

014
#014

Millennium Bridge — Subdued Sunrise

Nearly identical in framing to the previous shot but with the sun slightly lower, creating a warmer yellow glow at the upper right. Two small dark objects — possibly moorings or debris — float in the foreground water. The scene is tranquil, the city's morning rhythm not yet established.

File  IMG_2703.jpg

015
#015

Millennium Bridge — Brooding Sky

Storm clouds gather overhead, pushing the palette into a dramatic slate-grey. The Millennium Bridge is a dark silhouette against the lighter river. Canary Wharf emerges from the haze with unusual clarity as the light changes. Two small barges are visible in the river foreground.

File  IMG_2704.jpg

016
#016

Southwark Bridge — Stormy Morning

The most dramatic sky in the entire series — heavy cloud formations with a glowing lemon break at the horizon. Southwark Bridge spans the foreground in sharp silhouette. Tower Bridge in the distance appears particularly well-defined. The mood is cinematic and slightly ominous.

File  IMG_2705.JPG

017
#017

Southwark Bridge — Storm Clearing

The storm clouds begin to part, revealing a lime-green hazy horizon. The composition opens up as the bridge drops lower in the frame, giving more space to the sky. Tower Bridge catches the improving light and stands with quiet authority. The river traffic has thinned to almost nothing.

File  IMG_2706.JPG

018
#018

Tower Bridge — Wide Vista

The final and most expansive frame, pulling back to show the full stretch of the river from Southwark Bridge to Tower Bridge and beyond. The sky dominates — enormous and pale green — while Tower Bridge sits perfectly framed at the end of the river corridor. A fitting closing image: the city in full light, the morning established.

File  IMG_2707.JPG

Thames — Morning Light  ·  Eighteen Photographs London  ·  2025
